useCases.title: PR-to-Task Auto-linking
useCases.summary: Developer's PR automatically links to and closes the associated task on merge
useCases.description: Developer's PR automatically links to and closes the associated task on merge

scenario.context: Developer finishes coding a feature and creates a PR
scenario.before: Developer manually updates Jira ticket, copies PR link, changes status
scenario.after: PR description mentions task ID, merge auto-closes the task, team sees update in real-time
scenario.impact: Eliminates 5 minutes of manual bookkeeping per PR, ensures task board accuracy

useCases.trigger: Developer creates a pull request with a task ID reference in the description
preconditions: GitHub App is installed on the repository
Developer has linked their GitHub account to TaskFlow
postconditions: Task linked to PR with clickable reference
Task moved to Done on merge
Real-time board updated for all viewers

mainFlow.action: Developer creates a PR with 'Fixes TF-123' in the description
GitHub webhook notifies TaskFlow of the PR
System links the PR to task TF-123 and shows it on the task card
System automatically moves task TF-123 to Done

alternativeFlows.name: Multiple Tasks Referenced
steps: PR description references multiple task IDs (e.g., 'Fixes TF-123, TF-124')
System links PR to all referenced tasks
On merge, all referenced tasks move to Done

exceptionFlows.name: Invalid Task ID
exceptionFlows.trigger: PR references a task ID that doesn't exist in TaskFlow
exceptionFlows.handling: System ignores the invalid reference and logs a warning. PR comment bot adds a note about the unrecognized ID.

businessRules: Only merged PRs trigger task completion (not closed/abandoned)
Task ID pattern is case-insensitive (TF-123, tf-123, Tf-123 all match)
useCases.notes: This is the #1 requested feature from our developer beta testers.
